title: strychnos ledermannii
intro: Strychnos ledermannii, also known as the Ledermann's strychnine tree, is a species of plant in the Loganiaceae family. The plant is a small tree or shrub that is native to the tropical rainforests of Central Africa. Strychnos ledermannii contains compounds with medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make infusions or decoctions that are believed to provide health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
